codeReview.summary: The feature substantially improves the Work root by merging chat and terminal sessions, adding explicit root-state notices, and making new chat creation lane-aware, but it does not fully preserve the promised filter context because the Activity section ignores the active search and lane/status filters. It also leaves newly broadened live-session copy chat-specific even when the live items are terminal sessions.

issues.description: `activityFeed` is built from `liveChatSessions`, and `liveChatSessions` is derived from `mergedSessions` rather than the already-filtered `displaySessions` (`WorkRootScreen.swift:124-145`). The root always renders that feed whenever it is non-empty (`WorkRootScreen.swift:190-196`), so applying a lane filter or search still leaves unrelated activity rows from other sessions visible above the filtered browser. That breaks the feature's stated requirement to preserve search/filter/lane context and means the Work root does not consistently honor the mobile triage filters promised by `VAL-WORK-001` and `VAL-WORK-009`.

After this feature expanded the root browser to include terminal sessions, the live banner copy still hard-codes chat-only wording (`"1 chat is live"`, `"X chats are live"`, `"X chats need input"`) at `WorkRootComponents.swift:107-114`, even though the count it receives comes from `liveSessions` and includes terminal rows (`WorkRootScreen.swift:101-108,170-186`). The browser remains functional, but the banner now mislabels terminal-only live work and weakens the scannability/clarity this feature was supposed to improve.

summary: Fail: the Work browser/root-state/new-chat refactor largely lands, but the Activity section bypasses the active Work filters and shows out-of-scope sessions, so the root does not fully preserve the promised triage context. Non-blocking: the live banner still speaks about chats even when the live rows are terminal sessions.
